text.title: Weekly Growth Review Rhythm Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Weekly Growth Review Rhythm. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Pick 5-7 metrics: 1 NSM (north-star), 2-3 leading indicators, 1-2 efficiency, 1 health. More = ignored.
rule.rationale: Cognitive science: working-memory limit is 5-7 items; dashboards with >9 items are skimmed.

rule.statement: Maintain a rolling log of ≥3 active hypotheses with status (in-flight, won, lost, parked). Hypotheses are the unit of growth, not 'campaigns'.
rule.rationale: Without hypothesis structure, growth ops devolves to vibes; explicit log creates compounding.

rule.statement: Each weekly review produces ≥1 decision: continue, kill, double-down, redesign. 'Wait and see' = no decision = drift.
rule.rationale: Forced decisions drive velocity; status-only reviews don't change behaviour.

rule.statement: Review at fixed slot weekly. Same day, same time. Skipping = drift.
rule.rationale: Habit-formation literature: flexible schedule is unkept schedule.

rule.statement: Quarterly: are these still the right 5-7 metrics? Promote, demote, retire. Static metric sets blind teams to new realities.
rule.rationale: Stage of business shifts metric relevance; quarterly revisit keeps the dashboard alive.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-weekly-growth-review-rhythm.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
